methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
GITAI, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $129M+, was valued using two triangulation methods against a peer set of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies (1 public, 8 private). The peer universe includes 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), with a median valuation of $1.6B and range of $300M to $10.0B.

Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.7x multiplier to the $129M total-raised proxy, implying $219M. Peer-median analysis directly applied the cohort median of $1.6B valuation. These two methods produced materially different results, reflecting the wide dispersion in robotics venture valuations at this stage and the methodological tension between capital-efficiency metrics and market comparables.

The triangulated range is $186M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $910M. The $1.4B spread reflects key uncertainties: the peer set spans from Series B to Series D, making stage-normalization imprecise; the $10.0B outlier (1X Technologies) materially influences the comp distribution; and GITAI's specific technology differentiation and market traction relative to named peers remain material valuation drivers not captured in this quantitative framework alone.
